description:
You are the release-manager for a small company that runs its staging/production
servers over SSH.  
For the next deployment you first need a **fresh, dedicated key-pair** and then
have to document exactly what was generated so that the team-lead can review
it later.

Perform the following on the terminal:

1. Generate a new **RSA** key-pair with  
   • 4096 bits  
   • **NO pass-phrase**  
   • Comment string set to **release-manager@$(hostname)**  
   The private key must be saved as  
   /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key  
   (so the public key goes to /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key.pub automatically).

2. Make sure the private key file permissions are 600 and the public key is 644.

3. Append the *single* public-key line to  
   /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys  
   (create the file if it does not exist).  
   – Do **not** add it twice.  
   – Keep any previously existing keys intact.

4. Write a short log file at  
   /home/user/deployment/ssh_summary.log  
   inside a (new, if needed) directory /home/user/deployment.

   The file **must contain exactly these five lines** (no extra blank lines,
   no leading/trailing spaces):

   Line-1: SSH Key Generation Summary  
   Line-2: ---------------------------  
   Line-3: Key Path: /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key  
   Line-4: Public Key Path: /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key.pub  
   Line-5: Fingerprint: <THE EXACT FINGERPRINT as reported by “ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key”>

   Example of what line-5 should look like (your fingerprint will differ):  
   Fingerprint: SHA256:h7Yl+abCocPNddHQ6wJc7OlnIkQ8zP9u7PZ1pYwqx4w

Success criteria (what the automated checker will verify):
• /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key      exists, is not empty, and has 600 perms.  
• /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key.pub  exists, is not empty, and has 644 perms.  
• /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys contains the deploy_key **exactly once**.  
• /home/user/deployment/ssh_summary.log exists and its 5 lines follow the
  format above with a fingerprint that matches the one of deploy_key.

(You do **not** have to show the fingerprint on the console; the log file is
sufficient.)
truth:
Pre-existing state (before the task begins):
• Directory /home/user/.ssh/ already exists and is writable.
• authorized_keys may or may not exist; if it exists it contains the line  
  "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB3j6Lm9vDGf/oldexamplekey== dev@old"  
  (end with newline).

Ground-truth expectations after successful completion:
1. /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key
   • regular file, permissions 600.
2. /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key.pub
   • regular file, permissions 644.
3. The single textual line of deploy_key.pub appears **once and only once**
   in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys; the original ed25519 key (if present)
   is still there.
4. /home/user/deployment/ssh_summary.log
   • regular file, permissions 644 (default ok)
   • contains exactly 5 lines, UTF-8, with the last line’s fingerprint string
     exactly matching the output of:
       ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/deploy_key | awk '{print $2}'
   • The checker will compute the fingerprint from the saved key and compare
     it to the value present after the literal text “Fingerprint: ” in line 5.
name: task_000000_a3e5a6e2
